summary: Build could not start from the required `npx create-react-on-rails-app` onboarding path because this environment cannot resolve npm hosts and does not have Rails installed locally. I verified the workspace is empty, confirmed Node and npm are present, confirmed `rails` is unavailable, and confirmed DNS failure against the npm registry. Without network access to fetch `create-react-on-rails-app` and without a local Rails installation, I could not safely create or verify the React on Rails Pro application.
friction: `npx create-react-on-rails-app --help` produced no output and did not complete.
`npx --yes create-react-on-rails-app eval_app` also hung before any scaffold output.
`npm view create-react-on-rails-app version` did not return, indicating package metadata could not be fetched.
`rails --version` reported that Rails is not installed on this system.
`curl -I https://registry.npmjs.org/create-react-on-rails-app` failed with `curl: (6) Could not resolve host: registry.npmjs.org`, confirming DNS/network access to npm is unavailable.
blockers: Required bootstrap dependency unavailable: `create-react-on-rails-app` cannot be fetched because `registry.npmjs.org` is not resolvable from this environment.
Required runtime dependency unavailable: Rails is not installed locally (`rails --version` reports Rails is not currently installed).
Because the task explicitly requires beginning with `npx create-react-on-rails-app`, proceeding by hand-building a different stack would not satisfy the scenario.
